title: SaaS Support Platform: 89% Inference Cost Reduction
subtitle: Fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B replacing GPT-4o for customer support automation
description: A B2B SaaS company was spending $38K/month on OpenAI API calls to power their customer support automation — answering tickets, classifying intent, and drafting replies. We fine-tuned a Llama 3.1 8B model on three years of their support history, deployed it on vLLM with two A10G instances, and reduced their monthly AI spend to $4,200. P95 latency dropped from 2.1s to 680ms.

portfolio.projectSummary: The client's support automation was built on GPT-4o — fast to build, expensive to run. At 1.2M API calls per month with an average of 800 tokens per call, their bill had grown to $38K/month with no ceiling in sight. We fine-tuned a Llama 3.1 8B model on their support corpus using LoRA adapters via Unsloth, benchmarked it against GPT-4o on 2,000 held-out tickets, and deployed it on two AWS A10G instances via vLLM. The fine-tuned model matched or exceeded GPT-4o on their specific task distribution (intent classification, reply drafting, ticket routing) in 96% of test cases, while running at $0.08/M tokens vs $15/M. The full migration took seven weeks from audit to production.

challenges.title: Quality Bar Without Regression
challenges.description: The client needed confidence that the fine-tuned model wouldn't introduce quality regressions on edge cases — unusual ticket types, angry customers, multi-language queries. We built a benchmark of 2,000 stratified test cases covering the long tail of their ticket distribution and required 95%+ parity before signing off on production deployment.

challenges.title: Latency Requirements
challenges.description: The existing GPT-4o setup averaged 2.1s P95 latency, which was already a UX concern. The self-hosted vLLM deployment needed to match or beat this. Through continuous batching configuration and quantization to INT8, we achieved 680ms P95 — a 3x improvement alongside the 89% cost reduction.

portfolio.outcome: The client now runs their support AI on infrastructure they own and control. Monthly AI spend: $4,200. Annual savings vs. prior setup: approximately $405,000.
